title: spiraea convexa
intro: Spiraea convexa, also known as the rose mealy willow, is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Spiraea convexa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
